evals.prompt:
Build and validate the navigation README for a topic from its MIF findings. Run exactly:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-topic --findings $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-topic --findings $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md --check

Confirm the README has the required sections, the finding and source counts match the substrate, and the structural check passes.
evals.expected_output: README.md with an H1 title, a metadata header (Research ID, Created/Updated, Findings/Sources), and Purpose, Dimensions, Key Findings, Reports, and Tags sections. Findings is 3 and Sources is 2 (computed from the sample substrate, not hand-authored). The --check gate exits 0.

deterministic_checks.description: counts are computed from the substrate (3 findings, 2 distinct source URLs)
the README carries a Key Findings section
the README carries a Dimensions section
the structural validation gate passes (sections present, counts match, no dangling links)

expectations: The README is a navigation index, not a MIF report — it has an H1 title and no MIF frontmatter.
Findings and Sources counts are projected from the findings substrate, never invented.
The structural check passes before the README is reported reconciled.

evals.prompt:
The validation gate must fail closed when a README is missing or drifted. Demonstrate it. Run:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-topic --out $OUTDIR/none.md --check ; echo "check exit: $?"

The check must exit non-zero (the README does not exist), so a missing/invalid README can never be reported as reconciled.
evals.expected_output: build-topic-readme.sh --check exits non-zero because reports/none.md does not exist; the gate fails closed.

deterministic_checks.description: the validation gate exits non-zero for a missing README (fails closed)

expectations: The check is a real gate, not advisory — a missing or invalid README returns non-zero.
The agent reports the failure honestly rather than claiming the README shipped.

evals.prompt:
A just-created topic with zero findings must still get a valid README (the 'created' trigger). Run:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  mkdir -p $OUTDIR/empty-findings
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-topic --findings $OUTDIR/empty-findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-topic --findings $OUTDIR/empty-findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md --check

Confirm the zero-findings README is valid and passes the check.
evals.expected_output: A README with Findings: 0, a 'No findings yet' Key Findings bullet, and all required sections; the --check gate passes.

deterministic_checks.description: a created-but-unstarted topic reports zero findings/sources
empty state is handled without erroring

deterministic_checks.command: mkdir -p empty-findings && bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh" example-topic --findings empty-findings --out README.md --check
deterministic_checks.description: the zero-findings README passes the structural gate

expectations: The 'created' trigger produces a valid README even before any research has run.
Empty/partial state never errors the build or the check.
